verbGlossary.LOGGED AGAINST: {source} recorded in reference to the specific {target} where the issue was found on site
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS RESCHEDULE OF: {source} finding causes a tracked {target} change requiring activity re-sequencing or re-duration
verbGlossary.HALTS WORK ON: {source} stops active work on a critical-path {target} until the hazard is resolved
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED AS CAUSE OF: {source} formally categorized as the root cause of {target} delay event
verbGlossary.RECORDED DURING: {source} documented while the corresponding {target} is in progress
verbGlossary.CONSUMES FLOAT ON: {source} resolution time eats into available {target} on the affected activity path
verbGlossary.GATES HANDOVER AT: {source}must be cleared before the handover {target} can be achieved
verbGlossary.IMPOSES CONSTRAINT ON: Environmental or commissioning finding adds a mandatory {target} that must be satisfied before proceeding
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED FOR: {source} event placed on {target} tied to a specific detail activity's completion or readiness
verbGlossary.ANCHORED TO: {source} date fixed relative to {target} dates for contractual or regulatory compliance
verbGlossary.EVALUATES WORK IN: {source} checks the quality or conformance of work performed within a specific {target}
verbGlossary.BLOCKS COMPLETION OF: {source}inspection item prevents {target} from being marked complete until the deficiency is corrected
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED IN: {target}d inspection positioned within the look-ahead schedule to coordinate inspector availability with field readiness
verbGlossary.BOOKED PER: {target}d inspection date set according to the project work calendar's available working days
verbGlossary.MUST PRECEDE: {source}inspection must be completed and passed before the downstream {target} can proceed
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source}inspection requirement creates a hard constraint on {target} that cannot be overridden
verbGlossary.GENERATED FROM: {source} created as a deficiency found during walkthrough of a completed {target}
verbGlossary.HOLDS CLOSE-OUT OF: Open punch item prevents a milestone (typically substantial completion) from being formally closed
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED IN: {source} recorded in the as-built {target} record to reflect actual field conditions at completion
verbGlossary.FORCES RECOVERY IN: {source} issue category indicates rework scope significant enough to trigger a recovery {target}
verbGlossary.EXTENDS: {source} issue adds duration to {target} by requiring unplanned remediation work
verbGlossary.RESOLVED WITHIN: {source} resolution completed within the remaining duration window of the affected {target}
verbGlossary.CLOSED AS OF: {source} formally closed and verified as of {target} data date for period-end reporting
verbGlossary.CAUSES DELAY PER: {source} results in {target} delay that is formally classified by type (excusable, compensable, etc.)
verbGlossary.SHUTS DOWN: {source} forces an immediate shutdown of critical-path work until the site is cleared for re-entry
verbGlossary.ASSESSED FOR IMPACT VIA: {source}'s effect on project duration evaluated through a formal time impact analysis
verbGlossary.FILED AS OF: {source} date-stamped against {target} data date for accurate period attribution
verbGlossary.REFERENCED IN: {source} cited as supporting evidence in a retrospective delay analysis or claims proceeding
verbGlossary.TESTIFIES ABOUT WORK IN: Witness statement describes conditions during a specific {target} relevant to the incident
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO ACTIVITY IN: {source} task linked to {target} where the remediation work must be performed
verbGlossary.INCORPORATED INTO: {source} activities folded into the next {target} with durations and resources
verbGlossary.JUSTIFIES: {source} serves as contractual justification for a time extension request
verbGlossary.NECESSITATES: {source} requires {target} acceleration (overtime, added crews) to recover lost time after a stop-work event
verbGlossary.REJECTS WORK IN: {source} report formally rejects completed work in {target}, requiring tear-out and redo
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS RECOVERY VIA: {source} finding is severe enough to initiate a recovery {target} to maintain the completion date
verbGlossary.STOPS PROGRESS ON: {source} notice halts further progress on the affected {target} until the deficiency is cured
verbGlossary.DRIVES REVISION OF: {source} notice forces a revision to {target} to accommodate remediation and re-inspection time
verbGlossary.DISRUPTS LOGIC IN: {source}issue invalidates an assumed predecessor-successor relationship in {target} logic
verbGlossary.RESOLVED BEFORE: {source}issue must be resolved prior to the next {target} to avoid cascading delays
verbGlossary.ANALYZED FOR DELAY IN: {source}issue evaluated via time impact analysis to quantify its {target} effect
verbGlossary.RECLASSIFIES: {source}issue changes a dependency from discretionary to mandatory or vice versa
verbGlossary.REQUIRED BEFORE: {source}test report must be submitted and accepted before the associated construction {target} can proceed
verbGlossary.SPANS CURE TIME IN: {source}test report covers a curing or testing period that occupies lag time in {target} logic
verbGlossary.VALIDATES READINESS AT: {source}performance test confirms that building systems meet criteria required to achieve the commissioning {target}
verbGlossary.PRECONDITIONS: {source}performance test result is a prerequisite constraint that must be satisfied before {target} can advance
verbGlossary.DRIVES TASKS IN: {source} generates remediation {target}s that are inserted into the recovery schedule
verbGlossary.REFLECTED IN: {source} commitments captured in {target} update so planned remediation is visible in the project timeline
verbGlossary.MAPPED TO ACTIVITY IN: {source} line item linked to a specific {target} so the remediation task inherits dates and resources
verbGlossary.TRACKED ALONGSIDE: {source} task progress monitored in parallel with {target}'s duration and percent complete
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source} density metric analyzed alongside {target} performance index to detect quality-driven delays
verbGlossary.PREDICTS: {source}rate serves as a leading indicator that {target} on affected paths will erode
verbGlossary.INVERSELY IMPACTS: Higher punch item density corresponds to lower milestone adherence as close-out activities accumulate
verbGlossary.DEGRADES: {source}recordable incident rate weakens overall {target} health through stop-work events and investigation time
verbGlossary.CONCENTRATES ON: {source}rate incidents cluster around critical activities where {target} pressure increases safety risk
verbGlossary.ERODES: {source}systematically reduce {target} performance through repeated work stoppages
verbGlossary.INFLATES VARIANCE IN: {source}cost percentage increases the gap between earned {target} and actual progress
verbGlossary.DETERMINES REMAINING: {source} resolution time directly controls how much {target} remains on the affected activity's path
verbGlossary.INFLUENCES: {source}inspection pass rate supports stronger baseline execution index through fewer rework-driven re-{target}s
verbGlossary.SIGNALS RISK IN: Elevated near-miss ratio on out-of-sequence activities indicates rushed work with higher safety and {target} risk
verbGlossary.AFFECTS: {source} closure rate impacts percent plan complete by governing how quickly remediation {target}s finish
